a:r.a:t: Marketing Representative | External Compensation Plan Assessment
Total Compensation Range (i.e., Leverage / Actual potential earnings)
Entry-level earnings are typically lower but scale quickly with tenure and lead quality, reinforcing pay-for-performance culture
Other Metrics: Show Rate (%), Sales Conversion Rate
Customer Satisfaction sometimes used as a qualifier / bonus gate
5-8 tiered levels with progressively higher payouts as performance increases across tiers
with progressively higher payouts as performance increases across tiers
Weekly / Bi-weekly and monthly components (with occasional quarterly true-ups)
SPIFFs: Drive immediate behavior changes (same-day/over few days)
Budgets are small and typically a fixed dollar amount
STIs: Run for longer durations (up to ~3 months) to encourage behavior not built into the plan
Directors / VPs control SPIF/STI budgets but approval is required prior to launch
Ad hoc SPIFFs used by managers to provide additional incentives for priority tours, off-season periods, and short-term performance pushes
Contests, prizes, time-off perks, and experiential rewards used to boost engagement and retention
HGV is generally focused on number of tours booked and packages sold

whereas market plans generally place primary emphasis on tour generation / show-ups, with some plans also rewarding downstream purchase outcomes and, in certain cases, customer satisfaction or conversion quality gates.
HGV utilizes a more complex structure
(5–8 tiers) vs. the market standard of 3–5 tiers, potentially reducing clarity and line-of-sight for reps.
Competitors are showing a greater emphasis on non-monetary rewards
(contests, prizes, experiential incentives) alongside targeted SPIFFs.
Where HGV Varies from the Market
*Represents actual earnings data from 1/1/2025 through 12/31/2025, however does not represent mid-year job changes, and therefore might not be accurate for every employee
